formations_blocks: Both teams applying pressure on the ball
adjustments: Schlotterbeck struggling and appears set to come off early
Cote d’Ivoire looking to break quickly when Germany lose possession
key_matchups: Kimmich vs Fofana (crosses/shot save after build-up pressure)
Musiala vs Singo (Singo foul on Musiala)
Havertz vs Fofana (early shot and later header saved)
Diomande vs German defensive cover on the left
phases.summary: Germany start on the front foot, dominating possession and immediately testing Cote d’Ivoire with direct balls and wide delivery, while the Ivorians look dangerous whenever they can spring forward. The early pattern is one of duelling presses: Germany pressure high and force errors, but Cote d’Ivoire also press aggressively enough to rattle Fofana and create quick counters down the left through Diomande. Schlotterbeck’s early injury and impending substitution add an early defensive disruption for Germany.

formations_blocks: Germany increasing tempo in possession and pressing higher after a slow start
Cote d'Ivoire becoming slicker and more confident after scoring, likely operating in a more compact defending-and-break setup
adjustments: Germany need to adapt after falling behind
Potential change to help out Kimmich at right-back
Rüdiger appears set to come on for Schlotterbeck after the ankle issue
key_matchups: Diomande vs Germany on the left
Diallo vs Brown in front of goal
phases.summary: Germany gradually raised the pace and fluidity of their build-up, creating a couple of promising moments, but they were unable to convert pressure into a clean breakthrough. Cote d'Ivoire weathered that spell, then hit back with a direct left-side attack for Kessié’s goal, which shifted the game toward a more confident and dangerous Ivorian phase.

formations_blocks: Germany deep/under pressure after the restart
Ivory Coast playing with high energy and sustained attacking pressure
adjustments: Rudiger on for Schlotterbeck at the start of the second half
phases.summary: Germany went into halftime trailing 0-1, with the brief noting the general context rather than detailing a specific shape. After the break, Ivory Coast were described as overrunning Germany with repeated attacks, while Germany’s only clear change mentioned was the defensive substitution of Rudiger for Schlotterbeck. The commentary frames Ivory Coast as the more urgent side, pressing and attacking as if they were the team needing a goal.

formations_blocks: Germany struggle to impose control and are forced into a more reactive, unsettled defensive shape as Côte d’Ivoire’s pace and power dominate midfield.
Côte d’Ivoire are described as pushing Germany back and attacking with pace through midfield, implying a high-tempo, front-foot block rather than a passive one.
adjustments: Nagelsmann is plotting a triple change around the hour mark, indicating a planned tactical intervention.
Germany are urged to 'get hold of the ball more and calm things down,' suggesting a shift in emphasis from end-to-end play toward possession control.
key_matchups: Diallo vs Germany’s back line, especially Tah, in transition and around the box.
Kessié vs Germany’s midfield/defensive line as he drives Côte d’Ivoire forward.
Tah vs Oulai/Diallo in close-range defensive situations.
Musiala vs Côte d’Ivoire’s defensive coverage when he cuts inside to shoot.
Fofana vs Germany’s attackers in dealing with crosses and loose touches.
Nmecha vs Kessié, with Nmecha nicking possession after Kessié’s poor touch.
phases.summary: This phase is defined by Côte d’Ivoire imposing greater physical and running power in midfield, repeatedly unsettling Germany and forcing them into hurried clearances and loose possession. Germany do create a few moments through Musiala, Sane and set pieces, but the overall tactical story is one of Nagelsmann needing a major reset as the game tilts toward Cote d’Ivoire’s intensity before Germany equalize through Undav in the 68th minute.

formations_blocks: game became disjointed after the hydration break
Germany forced into a change of shape after Havertz went off for Goretzka
adjustments: 75': Germany and Cote d'Ivoire both made triple changes
82': Singo's hamstring issue led to Doue replacing him
85': Cote d'Ivoire brought on Pepe for Diomande
85': Germany replaced Havertz with Goretzka, prompting a shape change
key_matchups: Pepe vs a couple of Germans
phases.summary: This phase was broken up by substitutions, stoppages and a loss of rhythm after the hydration break, with neither side sustaining control for long spells. Cote d'Ivoire carried a real threat from set pieces and dribbling transitions, while Germany’s late change of shape and Undav’s direct running helped them finish strongly before scoring deep in stoppage time.
